question: How do I make a chat completion request with Eden AI?
expected_output: Send a POST request to https://api.edenai.run/v3/llm/chat/completions with an Authorization: Bearer header and Content-Type: application/json. The JSON body needs a 'model' field in provider/model format (e.g. openai/gpt-4) and a 'messages' array with role/content objects. The response follows the OpenAI format with choices[0].message.content containing the answer.

question: How do I extract text from a document using OCR with Eden AI?
expected_output: Use the Universal AI endpoint POST https://api.edenai.run/v3/universal-ai with a model string in the format ocr/ocr/provider (e.g. ocr/ocr/amazon). Provide the document as a file ID or URL in the input.file field and a language code in input.language. The response contains extracted text and bounding boxes.

question: How do I get structured JSON output from an LLM with Eden AI?
expected_output: Use the response_format parameter in your chat completions request. Two modes: json_object (set response_format to {"type": "json_object"} and instruct JSON in your prompt) and json_schema (provide a JSON Schema under response_format.json_schema with name, strict, and schema fields). The content field is still a string that needs parsing with json.loads(). Always include a message instructing JSON output.

question: What is the sandbox mode in Eden AI and how do I use it?
expected_output: Sandbox mode uses a sandbox_api_token instead of a production api_token. Requests made with a sandbox token return simulated mock responses that match the real response structure, at no cost and without real provider calls. Create sandbox tokens from the Eden AI dashboard under API Keys or via the custom token API.

question: How does caching work in Eden AI?
expected_output: Eden AI automatically caches responses for identical requests (same model and input). Cached responses are returned immediately at no cost. Caching works best for deterministic features like embeddings, moderation, OCR, and NER. It is less useful for non-deterministic outputs like LLM chat and image generation. Caching is enabled by default and can be toggled per project in the dashboard.

question: How do I list available LLM models in Eden AI?
expected_output: Send GET to /v3/llm/models with your Authorization header. The response contains a data array where each item has id (the model string, e.g. 'openai/gpt-4o'), owned_by (provider name), and a capabilities object with boolean fields: pdf, reasoning, web_search, and tool_calling. Use the model id directly as the model parameter in requests.

question: How do I use BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) with Eden AI?
expected_output: In the Eden AI dashboard, navigate to provider settings, select a provider (e.g. OpenAI, Google, Anthropic), enter your own provider API key, and save. Requests to that provider then use your key and are billed directly by the provider. You still get Eden AI's unified API, monitoring, routing, and fallback. You can mix BYOK for some providers with Eden AI billing for others.

question: How do I enable web search for an LLM request?
expected_output: Add web_search_options to your chat completions request with search_context_size set to 'low', 'medium', or 'high' (default: 'medium'). Not all models support web search -- check capabilities.web_search from GET /v3/llm/models. The model automatically searches the web for relevant information and generates a grounded response.

question: How do I use Eden AI with Claude Code?
expected_output: Set environment variables: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to your Eden AI API key and ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to https://api.edenai.run/v3/llm. Optionally set ANTHROPIC_MODEL to a provider/model string (e.g. 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5').

question: How do I upload a file and use it in an LLM conversation?
expected_output: Upload via POST /v3/upload as multipart with the 'file' field. The response returns a file_id, file_name, file_size, and expires_at (7-day default retention). Reference the file in chat completions messages using content array format: [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}, {"type": "file", "file": {"file_id": "..."}}]. Max file size is 100 MB.

question: How does smart routing work in Eden AI, and how do I use it?
expected_output: Set model to '@edenai' in your chat completions request. Eden AI's router analyzes your request and selects the optimal model. Optionally provide router_candidates to restrict selection to specific models.

question: How do I use the Responses API with Eden AI?
expected_output: POST to /v3/llm/responses with model (provider/model format), input (string or array), and optionally instructions (system prompt). Responses are stored server-side by default (store: true). For multi-turn, chain with previous_response_id. Response text is at output[0].content[0].text. Important: stateful features (storage, retrieval, previous_response_id) only work with providers that natively support the Responses API.

question: How do I process a large multi-page PDF with OCR using Eden AI?
expected_output: Use the async OCR endpoint: POST /v3/universal-ai/async with model 'ocr/ocr_async/provider' (e.g. ocr/ocr_async/amazon). Provide the file as a file ID or URL in the input. The initial response returns a job_id. Poll GET /v3/universal-ai/async/{job_id} until the job completes. The output contains raw_text, pages array with lines and words including bounding boxes and confidence scores.

question: How do I use webhooks to get async job results instead of polling?
expected_output: Add a 'webhook_receiver' field with your endpoint URL to any async Universal AI request (POST /v3/universal-ai/async). When the job completes, Eden AI sends a POST to your URL with JSON containing job_id, status ('success' or 'fail'), cost, provider, feature, subfeature, output (on success), and error (on failure). Your endpoint must return 200 to acknowledge receipt.

question: How do I pass provider-specific parameters to an expert model request?
expected_output: Add a 'provider_params' object to your Universal AI request alongside 'model' and 'input'. The contents are forwarded directly to the provider without validation by Eden AI. For example, for DALL-E 3 image generation, set provider_params to {"quality": "hd", "style": "vivid"}. Available parameters vary by provider and are not listed in Eden AI docs -- refer to the provider's own documentation.

question: How do I create a custom API token with a budget limit?
expected_output: POST to /v2/user/custom_token/ with name, token_type ('api_token' or 'sandbox_api_token'), balance (string, e.g. '50.00'), and active_balance set to true. When active_balance is true, every API call deducts from the token's balance and the token stops working at $0. List tokens with GET /v2/user/custom_token/, update with PATCH, delete with DELETE.

question: How do I migrate my existing OpenAI code to use Eden AI?
expected_output: Change two things in your OpenAI client: set api_key to your Eden AI API key and base_url to https://api.edenai.run/v3/llm. Change model strings from 'gpt-4' to 'openai/gpt-4' (provider/model format). All OpenAI SDK methods (chat.completions.create, streaming, multi-turn, vision) work the same way. You can also use non-OpenAI models like 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5'.

question: What model string format do I use for LLMs vs expert models?
expected_output: LLMs use provider/model format (e.g. 'openai/gpt-4o', 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5'). Expert models use feature/subfeature/provider format, optionally with a model variant: feature/subfeature/provider/model (e.g. 'text/moderation/openai', 'ocr/ocr/amazon', 'image/generation/openai/dall-e-3'). LLM models go with POST /v3/llm/chat/completions. Expert model strings go with POST /v3/universal-ai.

question: What is the difference between the fallback syntax for LLMs and expert models?
expected_output: Both use a 'fallbacks' array in the request body, but the model string format differs. For LLMs: POST /v3/llm/chat/completions, model and fallbacks use provider/model format (e.g. 'openai/gpt-4o'). For expert models: POST /v3/universal-ai, model and fallbacks use feature/subfeature/provider format (e.g. 'text/moderation/google'). All fallback providers must support the same feature.

question: What is the difference between listing models for LLMs vs expert models?
expected_output: LLMs use GET /v3/llm/models which returns a data array with model IDs in provider/model format and a capabilities object (pdf, reasoning, web_search, tool_calling). Expert models use GET /v3/info (all features) and GET /v3/info/{feature}/{subfeature} (specific feature) which returns providers, model strings in feature/subfeature/provider format, pricing, and input/output schemas. Different endpoints, different response structures.

question: Does streaming work with expert models (Universal AI endpoint)?
expected_output: No, streaming is only available for LLM requests via POST /v3/llm/chat/completions with stream: true. The Universal AI endpoint (POST /v3/universal-ai) does not support streaming. For long-running expert model tasks, use the async endpoint POST /v3/universal-ai/async which returns a job_id for polling or webhook delivery.

question: How do I use expert models in Eden AI?
expected_output: Expert models use the Universal AI endpoint: POST /v3/universal-ai. The model string format is feature/subfeature/provider (e.g. ocr/ocr/amazon, text/moderation/google). For async tasks like large OCR or audio transcription, use POST /v3/universal-ai/async.
